untranslatable_reason: BARR pattern requires geometric construction of parallel trendlines (warning line, sell line) at specific intervals relative to lead-in trend, and identification of 'bump phase' height measurement—these structural elements cannot be expressed with available point-in-time indicators. The pattern relies on drawing parallel lines at measured intervals, which requires trend line regression and relative positioning not available in the indicator set. Approximation above uses overbought/volume/trend conditions but loses the core geometric definition.
